subject: Chemistry
title: Mauveine: The Accidental Purple
description: A quick win: understand Mauveine: The Accidental Purple.

hook.text: In 1856, an 18-year-old chemistry student accidentally made the world's first synthetic dye while trying to cure malaria.

buildup.text: William Perkin was attempting to synthesize quinine from coal tar. His experiment produced a dark sludge instead.

discovery.text: The sludge dyed silk a vivid purple that didn't fade. Perkin called it mauveine—the first aniline dye.

twist.text: Queen Victoria wore a mauveine-dyed dress to her daughter's wedding. Purple went from royal rarity to mass fashion.

climax.text: Perkin's accident launched a billion-dollar dye industry. By age 36, he was rich enough to retire and do pure research.

punchline.text: A failed malaria cure became the first synthetic dye. Chemistry's luckiest mistake.

quiz.question: How was mauveine discovered?
options: William Perkin accidentally created it while trying to synthesize quinine
A textile worker noticed coal tar stained fabric purple
French chemists deliberately designed it from aniline
